_comment: Governed bridge: reviewer-manifest binding_key -> showcase ontology module(s).
load_query_bindings) to resolve a Selector v 1.0 ground-truth query pick to the OBDA
mappings that republish its governed story. EXPLICIT ONLY — no fuzzy SQL matching:
a query or key absent from this file renders as 'no ontology mapping bound' (fail visible).
bindings: binding_key -> [showcase module]. Each entry is defensible from the POC docs:
- three_way_match keys are named in three_way_match_parity_check.py / README Showcase 10
- operations_oeeoperational is the operational OEE metric of README Showcase 5
- operations_deliveryperformanceops is the ops on-time story (purchase_order+receiving+suppliers) of Showcases 1-4
- suppliers_supplierscorecard carries the performance_rating recomputed in README Showcase 3
query_bindings: governed palette query name -> [binding_key]. Supplies the query->key hop
ONLY for governed queries that carry no '-- Binding:' marker in their palette file (a marker,
when present, always wins). 'Three-Way Match Exceptions' interrogates the same strict
po_line -> receiving_line -> payable_line linkage whose received/unreceived governed views
ground the three_way_match showcase parity check.
Modules whose grounding queries are ARCHIVED (capacity_planning, customer_order_demand,
inventory_transactions, shop_floor_routing) have no APPROVED binding key and stay unbound;
they remain reachable through the flat browse-all picker.
